demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
Refactor ChurnPilot to remove remaining Anthropic/Claude-based AI usage and standardize all AI calls on `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

## Context
We inspected current ChurnPilot runtime usage and found that Anthropic is still active in production code, even though the intended direction is OpenRouter-only.

Current state:
- `src/core/ask_ai.py` already uses OpenRouter only
- `src/core/health.py` already checks OpenRouter liveness only
- `src/core/pipeline.py` still uses `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `CLAUDE_API_KEY` in `_extract_with_claude()`
- `src/core/importer.py` still resolves `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` as primary but falls back to Anthropic/Claude
- `requirements.txt` still includes `anthropic>=0.39.0`
- Multiple docs/tests/scripts still mention `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and Claude fallback behavior

This means ChurnPilot is not yet truly OpenRouter-only. We want to eliminate Anthropic entirely so runtime config, docs, and tests all align on `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

## Requirements
- [ ] Remove remaining runtime reads of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `CLAUDE_API_KEY`
- [ ] Refactor `src/core/pipeline.py` so extraction uses OpenRouter instead of Claude fallback/direct Claude path
- [ ] Refactor `src/core/importer.py` so spreadsheet import uses OpenRouter only (no Anthropic fallback)
- [ ] Remove Anthropic SDK dependency if no longer needed anywhere in runtime code
- [ ] Update environment examples, deployment docs, architecture docs, and any setup docs to reference `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` only
- [ ] Update tests/scripts that currently assume Anthropic/Claude behavior
- [ ] Verify no remaining Anthropic/Claude references remain in active ChurnPilot runtime paths

## Acceptance Tests
- `test_extract_from_url_uses_openrouter_only()` — extraction succeeds/fails based on OpenRouter config only; no Anthropic fallback path remains
- `test_extract_from_text_uses_openrouter_only()` — text extraction no longer reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `CLAUDE_API_KEY`
- `test_spreadsheet_importer_requires_openrouter_only()` — importer uses `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` only and no longer initializes Claude fallback state
- `test_health_check_reports_openrouter_status()` — health check remains OpenRouter-based and consistent with runtime behavior
- `test_no_anthropic_runtime_dependency()` — runtime code no longer imports Anthropic SDK or references Anthropic env vars

## Technical Notes
Relevant findings from inspection:
- `src/core/pipeline.py`
  - `_extract_with_ai()` currently falls back from Gemini to Claude
  - `_extract_with_claude()` reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `CLAUDE_API_KEY` from env or Streamlit secrets and imports `anthropic`
- `src/core/importer.py`
  - `SpreadsheetImporter` resolves `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` first, then Anthropic/Claude fallback
  - `_call_ai()` falls back to `_call_claude()`
  - `_call_claude()` imports `anthropic`
- `requirements.txt` still contains `anthropic>=0.39.0`
- Docs/tests/scripts with Anthropic references currently include:
  - `.env.example`
  - `docs/DEPLOY.md`
  - `docs/ENVIRONMENTS.md`
  - `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`
  - `docs/QUICK_SUMMARY.md`
  - `docs/PLATFORM_INDEPENDENCE_AUDIT.md`
  - `tests/test_importer.py`
  - `scripts/model_comparison_test.py`
  - `docs/TEST_HEALTH_REPORT.md`

Suggested approach:
1. Convert pipeline extraction to OpenRouter-backed implementation
2. Remove Claude fallback from importer
3. Remove Anthropic dependency/config/docs/tests in one cleanup pass
4. Run focused regression tests for extraction, spreadsheet import, and Ask AI lookup
demos.dispatch_note:
**CTO Dispatch — 2026-03-07 14:20 PST**

Dispatching backend engineer to remove all remaining Anthropic/Claude runtime references and dependencies, fully standardizing on `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` usage.

Key tasks:
- Remove Anthropic imports and env var reads from `pipeline.py`, `importer.py`, and other runtime files.
- Refactor extraction flows to use only OpenRouter (remove Claude fallback).
- Remove `anthropic` SDK dependency from `requirements.txt`.
- Update documentation and test scripts to drop all Anthropic/Claude mentions.
- Validate with acceptance tests ensuring no runtime Anthropic dependency and extraction runs solely on OpenRouter.
- Confirm health check is consistent with OpenRouter-only.

No further Anthropic fallback or SDK usage should remain anywhere in production code.

---

Priority: Medium (non-breaking refactor but critical for configuration consistency and future maintenance).

Code review and test coverage focused on runtime imports and extraction flow correctness.

demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
Create comprehensive test coverage for the complete trial registration and billing flow.

## Context
The trial billing system requires end-to-end validation covering registration, trial period, conversion, and cancellation scenarios.

## Requirements
- [ ] Test full registration with payment method collection
- [ ] Test trial period simulation (accelerated timeline)
- [ ] Test cancellation during trial (no charge)
- [ ] Test trial-to-paid conversion (billing starts day 31)
- [ ] Test payment failure during trial conversion
- [ ] Test email notification delivery
- [ ] Test feature gating during and after trial
- [ ] Test billing page state transitions

## Acceptance Tests
- `test_e2e_registration_with_trial()` — Complete registration, trial active
- `test_e2e_trial_cancellation()` — Cancel during trial, verify no charge
- `test_e2e_trial_conversion()` — Trial ends, payment succeeds, subscription active
- `test_e2e_trial_conversion_payment_fails()` — Trial ends, payment fails, status past_due
- `test_e2e_billing_page_all_states()` — Unpaid → Trial → Paid → Canceled flow
- `test_e2e_feature_access_trial_vs_expired()` — Pro access during trial, revoked after

## Technical Notes
- Use test-mode Stripe keys (sk_test_...)
- Use Stripe Test Clock for time simulation if available
- Test harness: Use existing scripts/stripe_test_harness.py patterns
- Database assertions for all state transitions

### Dependencies
- [x] #176
- [x] #177
demos.dispatch_note:

- Simulate time using Stripe Test Clock

- Assert DB row correctness for user, billing, and subscription states

All acceptance tests named in ticket expected.

Please include mocks where appropriate and keep tests fast and deterministic.

Upon completion, update ticket status to `status:review`.

demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
Parse Spreadsheet fails when uploading an Excel file (.xlsx) via the Upload File tab on the experiment endpoint.

**Severity:** 🟡 Major — core feature broken for file uploads

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Go to experiment endpoint → Add Card → Parse Spreadsheet
2. Select "Upload File" import method
3. Upload an .xlsx file (e.g., `Copy of 银行账户 - ChurnPilot Testing 2026_02_28.xlsx`, 15.9KB)
4. Click "Parse Spreadsheet"

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** Cards parsed and displayed for preview/import
- **Actual:** Error: "Unable to parse any cards from the spreadsheet. Please check the format and try again."
- **Error details:** `Critical error: Expecting value: line 373 column 14 (char 8875)`

## Technical Notes
- The error `Expecting value: line 373 column 14` looks like a JSON parse error — likely the OpenRouter API response is not being parsed correctly, or the LLM returned malformed JSON
- This may be related to CP #106 (OpenRouter integration) — the API call might succeed but the response parsing fails
- The file is a Chinese bank statement spreadsheet (银行账户), so non-ASCII content may be a factor
- CP #100 fixed a user_id issue in Parse Spreadsheet, but this is a different error (JSON parsing)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Upload File → Parse Spreadsheet works with the test .xlsx file
- [ ] Error handling shows useful message if parsing fails (not generic "check format")
- [ ] Non-ASCII content (Chinese characters) handled correctly
demos.dispatch_note:
📸 **Screenshot:** Parse Spreadsheet error on experiment endpoint

![Parse Spreadsheet error on experiment endpoint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hendrixAIDev/churn_copilot_hendrix/screenshots/issue-109-20260301-175357.png)
---
### CTO Triage — 2026-03-01 17:56 PST

**Priority:** HIGH (launch blocker — core feature broken)
**Classification:** Backend bug — JSON parsing failure in OpenRouter/AI extraction pipeline
**Evolver match:** None

**Analysis:**
- Error `Expecting value: line 373 column 14 (char 8875)` is a classic `json.JSONDecodeError`
- Likely the OpenRouter API response for .xlsx parsing includes non-JSON content (markdown fencing, error text, or malformed output)
- Possibly related to CP #106 (OpenRouter integration) — response parsing may not handle edge cases
- Non-ASCII content (Chinese bank statement) could trigger different LLM output formatting

**Dispatch:** Backend engineer (Opus + medium thinking) — this is a parsing/API integration bug that needs careful tracing through the extraction pipeline.

**Branch:** `fix/cp109-xlsx-parse-error`
---
### 2026-03-01 18:05 — Implementation Complete
**Agent:** Backend Architect
**Status:** READY FOR REVIEW

## Root Cause

The `parse_spreadsheet()` method in `src/core/importer.py` used a brittle approach to extract JSON from AI responses:

```python
json_match = re.search(r'\[\s*\{.*\}\s*\]', response_text, re.DOTALL)
cards_data = json.loads(json_match.group(0))
```

When the AI (OpenRouter/Gemini) returned JSON wrapped in markdown code fences (e.g., ` ```json ... ``` `), the regex either failed to match or matched content including the fence markers, causing `json.JSONDecodeError` at parse time. This is especially common with non-ASCII content (Chinese bank statements) where models tend to add explanation text.

## Fix

Extracted a new `_extract_json_array()` helper function with a multi-step parsing strategy:

1. **Strip markdown fences** — regex to remove ` ```json ... ``` ` or ` ``` ... ``` ` wrapping
2. **Direct parse** — try `json.loads()` on the cleaned text (fastest path)
3. **Non-greedy regex** — extract `[{...}]` with non-greedy matching
4. **Greedy fallback** — original greedy pattern for deeply nested objects
5. **Clear error messages** — logs parse position on failure, raises descriptive `ValueError`

The `parse_spreadsheet()` method now calls `_extract_json_array(response_text)` instead of inline regex+json.loads.

Also moved `import json` and `import logging` to module level (was imported inline).

## Files Changed

- `src/core/importer.py` — Added `_extract_json_array()` helper, updated `parse_spreadsheet()` to use it, module-level imports
- `tests/test_importer.py` — Added 16 unit tests for `_extract_json_array()` + 2 integration tests through `parse_spreadsheet()`

## Diff Stat

```
src/core/importer.py   |  85 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
tests/test_importer.py | 132 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
2 files changed, 209 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)
```

## Test Results

```
751 passed, 32 skipped, 2 warnings in 4.86s
```

All exi
---
### 🔍 Code Review Report

**Ticket:** #109 — Parse Spreadsheet fails on Upload File with Excel (.xlsx)
**Reviewer:** Code Review Agent
**Verdict:** ✅ APPROVE

---

**Files reviewed:**
- `src/core/importer.py` — New `_extract_json_array()` helper with multi-step parsing strategy; `parse_spreadsheet()` now delegates to it; `import json`/`import logging` promoted to module level
- `tests/test_importer.py` — 16 unit tests for `_extract_json_array()` + 2 integration tests through `parse_spreadsheet()` (markdown-fenced response, Chinese non-ASCII)

---

**Gates:**

- ✅ **GATE 0 — Scope:** Diff touches exactly 2 files (`src/core/importer.py`, `tests/test_importer.py`), both squarely in scope
- ✅ **GATE 1 — Lint:** `ruff check` → All checks passed, zero violations

---

**Checks:**

- ✅ **Correctness** — Root cause identified precisely: brittle greedy regex + inline `json.loads` in `parse_spreadsheet()` faile

demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
Replace free-form `frequency` string on `Credit` model with a validated enum. Add normalization to catch AI extraction variations.

## Context
Currently `Credit.frequency` is a plain string. The period tracking system (`periods.py`) recognizes 4 values: monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, annual. Any other value silently falls through to annual. This is fragile — AI extraction could return "biweekly", "yearly", or "semi-annual" and the system would silently treat it as annual.

## Requirements
- [ ] Create `CreditFrequency` enum in `models.py`: `monthly`, `quarterly`, `semi_annually`, `annual`
- [ ] Add `normalize_frequency()` function that maps common variations:
  - "yearly" / "annually" → `annual`
  - "semi-annual" / "biannual" / "bi-annual" → `semi_annually`  
  - "every month" → `monthly`
  - "every quarter" → `quarterly`
- [ ] Add Pydantic field validator on `Credit.frequency` that auto-normalizes
- [ ] Update `periods.py` to use enum values instead of string comparisons
- [ ] Call `normalize_frequency()` in importer after AI extraction
- [ ] Update AI extraction prompt to list exact valid values
- [ ] Verify existing library templates use valid enum values
- [ ] Migration: scan existing DB data for non-standard frequency values

## Technical Notes
- Key files: `src/core/models.py`, `src/core/periods.py`, `src/core/importer.py`, `src/core/pipeline.py`
- Library templates in `src/core/library.py` already use standard values
- The enum should serialize as lowercase string for JSON/DB compatibility
- Pydantic v2 supports `@field_validator` with `mode="before"` for auto-normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Credit(frequency="yearly")` auto-normalizes to `annual`
- [ ] `Credit(frequency="invalid")` raises validation error
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] Period tracking works with enum values
- [ ] AI extraction output gets normalized before Card construction
demos.dispatch_note:
## CTO Triage — Round 1

**Priority:** Medium
**Classification:** Backend refactor — model validation + normalization
**Evolver:** No capsule match

**Analysis:** Well-scoped ticket. Creates a CreditFrequency enum, adds normalize_frequency() mapping, integrates with Pydantic v2 field_validator, and updates periods.py + importer. Low risk — mostly additive changes with clear acceptance criteria.

**Key files:** models.py, periods.py, importer.py, pipeline.py, library.py
**Dependencies:** None

**Dispatching:** Backend engineer (standard model, low thinking)
---
### 2026-03-01 19:45 — Implementation Complete
**Agent:** Backend Architect (engineer)
**Status:** READY FOR REVIEW

**What I did:**
1. Added `CreditFrequency` enum to `models.py` with 4 members: `MONTHLY`, `QUARTERLY`, `SEMI_ANNUALLY`, `ANNUAL`
2. Added `normalize_frequency()` function with alias map covering 20+ variations (yearly, biannual, semiannual, semi_annual, per year, etc.)
3. Added Pydantic `field_validator` on `Credit.frequency` that auto-normalizes on construction — any `Credit()` instantiation now gets normalized frequency
4. Updated `periods.py` to compare against `CreditFrequency` enum values instead of raw strings
5. Updated `importer.py` to call `normalize_frequency()` explicitly after AI extraction, with graceful fallback to "annual" on unknown values + warning log
6. Wrote 40 new tests covering enum values, normalization of all aliases, field_validator behavior, error paths, and periods integration

**Decisions made:**
- Used `str, Enum` pattern so enum members compare equal to their string values — zero breakage for existing code that passes plain strings
- Unknown frequencies raise `ValueError` in `normalize_frequency()` but importer catches this and falls back to "annual" with a warning (matching existing silent fallback behavior, but now logged)
- Kept `Credit.frequency` field type as `str` (not `CreditFrequency`) to maintain serialization compatibility with existing DB data

**Files changed:**
- `src/core/models.py` — Added `CreditFrequency` enum, `_FREQUENCY_ALIASES` dict, `normalize_frequency()`, field_validator on Credit
- `src/core/periods.py` — Import `CreditFrequency`, use enum values in comparisons
- `src/core/importer.py` — Import `normalize_frequency`, explicit normalization after AI extraction
- `tests/test_issue_111_credit_frequency.py` — 40 new tests

**Diff stat:**
```
 src/core/importer.py                     |  16 +++-
 src/core/models.py                       |  61 +++++++++++++++
 src/core/periods.py                      |  
---
### 🔍 Code Review Report

**Ticket:** #111 — Add CreditFrequency enum and normalize_frequency() for Credit model
**Reviewer:** Code Review Agent
**Verdict:** ✅ APPROVE

---

**Files reviewed:**
- `src/core/models.py` — Added `CreditFrequency(str, Enum)`, `_FREQUENCY_ALIASES` dict (20+ mappings), `normalize_frequency()`, and `field_validator` on `Credit.frequency`
- `src/core/periods.py` — Updated string comparisons to use `CreditFrequency` enum values
- `src/core/importer.py` — Added explicit `normalize_frequency()` call after AI extraction with graceful fallback + warning log
- `tests/test_issue_111_credit_frequency.py` — 40 new tests covering enum, normalization, field validator, and periods integration

**Checks:**
- [x] **Correctness** — Solves the ticket. Enum + normalization + validator all work correctly together.
- [x] **Edge cases** — Empty string, unknown values, whitespace trimming, case-insensitive matching all covered.
- [x] **Security** — No vulnerabilities. No exposed credentials, no injection risks.
- [x] **Error handling** — `normalize_frequency()` raises `ValueError` for unknown input; importer catches it gracefully and falls back to "annual" with a logged warning.
- [x] **Consistency** — Follows existing codebase patterns. Smart use of `str, Enum` so enum members compare equal to their string values — zero breakage for existing code.
- [x] **Performance** — Dict lookup for normalization

demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
On an account with no cards, selecting a card from the library (e.g. American Express Platinum) via "Add from Library" redirects back to the Dashboard tab instead of staying on Add Card.

**Severity:** 🟡 Major — Same class of bug as #99 (tab jump on rerun), but in the library selection flow.

**Environment:** ChurnPilot experiment (`churnpilot-experiment.streamlit.app`)

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Log in with an account that has no cards
2. Go to Add Card tab
3. Select "Add from Library"
4. Choose a card (e.g. American Express Platinum)
5. Page redirects to Dashboard tab

## Expected vs Actual
- **Expected:** Stay on Add Card tab, card selection proceeds (preview/confirm)
- **Actual:** Redirected to Dashboard tab

## Technical Notes
- Same class of issue as #99 (fixed with `@st.fragment` on `render_add_card_section`). The fragment fix prevents radio-button reruns from resetting tabs, but library card selection likely triggers a different rerun path (e.g. `st.rerun()` or state change outside the fragment scope).
- **Required:** After fixing Add from Library, audit ALL other Add Card sub-flows for the same issue:
  - [ ] Add from Library (this bug)
  - [ ] Manual Entry
  - [ ] Import from Spreadsheet / Paste CSV
  - [ ] AI Extraction (Upload File)
- Check if `st.rerun(scope="app")` calls in card-save paths are causing full tab resets
- The #99 fix added `scope="app"` to rerun calls — verify this is correct vs `scope="fragment"`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add from Library stays on Add Card tab after card selection
- [ ] All other Add Card sub-flows verified (no tab jump on any path)
- [ ] Test coverage for library card selection flow
- [ ] Regression test preventing tab-jump on all Add Card paths

- Related to #99 fix — may need `scope="fragment"` instead of `scope="app"` in certain paths.

Resetting to `status:new` for fresh triage and re-dispatch.

Resetting to `status:new` for fresh triage and re-dispatch.

### CTO Reset — 2026-03-01 09:11 PST

**What happened:** The board review cron timed out at 15 min before spawning the v2 engineer. The ticket appeared `status:in-progress` but no engineer was ever running.

**Resetting to `status:new`** for fresh dispatch on next board review pass.

### Engineer Guidance (v3)

- The bug: selecting a card from 'Add from Library' triggers a Streamlit rerun outside the `@st.fragment` scope, resetting the active tab to Dashboard

### CTO Triage — 2026-03-01 09:17 PST

**Status:** `status:new` → `status:in-progress` (dispatching engineer v3)

**Analysis:** v1 and v2 engineers both died without producing any commits. The board review cron timed out before spawning v2. This is attempt #3 with explicit guidance to create worktree first and commit early.

**Action:** Dispatched `eng-cp105-library-tab-v3` (backend engineer, 20 min timeout, sonnet).

**Key guidance for v3:**
- Create worktree from origin/experiment FIRST
- Commit early and often (don't die with uncommitted work)
- Focus: `src/ui/pages/card_management/add_card.py` — library card selection triggers rerun outside fragment scope
- Audit ALL Add Card sub-flows after fixing
---
## Fix: Add from Library stays on Add Card tab (ref #105)

### Root Cause

When a user with no cards clicks "Add Your First Card" from the empty Dashboard, `go_to_add_card()` sets `navigate_to_add_card=True`. On the next render, `app.py` consumes the flag (sets it to `False`) and renders `render_add_card_section()` **outside** the `st.tabs()` widget. When the user then selects a library card and clicks "Add Card", `st.rerun(scope='app')` fires. On the subsequent render, `navigate_to_add_card=False` — so the app falls through to `st.tabs()`. Because `st.tabs()` was absent on the previous render, React has no prior tab state and defaults to tab 1 (Dashboard). The user sees Dashboard instead of Add Card.

The `@st.fragment` decorator from #99 correctly prevents selectbox changes from causing tab jumps (confirmed: library dropdown selection triggers only fragment reruns). The bug was specifically in the `navigate

demos.ticket_body:
## Summary
Update the AI extraction prompts (both URL pipeline and spreadsheet importer) to strictly enforce the `CreditFrequency` enum values introduced in #111. Push normalization responsibility to the LLM instead of maintaining an ever-growing alias map.

## Context
CP #111 added `CreditFrequency` enum with a `_FREQUENCY_ALIASES` safety net. But the real fix is making the LLM output clean values from the start — there are too many natural language variations to enumerate all aliases ourselves.

## Requirements
- [ ] Update `EXTRACTION_PROMPT` in `src/core/pipeline.py` — change `frequency` field description to: `MUST be exactly one of: "monthly", "quarterly", "semi_annually", "annual". No other values. If the source uses different terms (e.g., "yearly", "every month", "bi-annual", "每月"), normalize to one of these four.`
- [ ] Update spreadsheet parsing prompt in `src/core/importer.py` — same strict enum constraint for benefit frequency
- [ ] Update the JSON example in both prompts to use `"semi_annually"` (underscore, matching enum)
- [ ] Add integration test: feed AI a benefit with "yearly" frequency, verify it comes back as "annual"
- [ ] Add integration test: feed AI a benefit with non-English frequency term, verify normalization

## Technical Notes
- `src/core/pipeline.py` — `EXTRACTION_PROMPT` (URL extraction)
- `src/core/importer.py` — spreadsheet parsing prompt (around line 303)
- The `_FREQUENCY_ALIASES` in models.py stays as a safety net but should rarely trigger after this change
- Both prompts use slightly different JSON schemas — update both consistently

### Dependencies

- [x] #111
